entry.title: Re: Bank Account Import from Import/Export Bank Account i...
entry.content:
Hi @Hnin_1234,

House banks are maintained with banks. You can use the Manage Banks - Cash Management app (F1574A) to edit a bank and create house banks that should be linked to the bank.

For more information, see: https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/186460fdc35a4b64a713da9bb00deb1e/a4e4d58b503d4ab69f63d7ec92f3b1f9.html?locale=en-US

entry.title: Re: SAP S/4HANA Cloud. Can I get access to my CDS view via OData4 interface - "sap/opu/odata4/s
entry.content:
I just happened across this today - explaining how to create CDS ODATA sources, and how to expose them as APIs...

https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-members/creating-odata-services-with-cds-using-sadl-and-annotation/ba-p/13558651

entry.title: Error while importing data in SAC model via excel file
entry.content:
Hello SAC experts,

I am relevantly new to SAC and need help.

I created a blank model in SAC and created dimensions and measures. I am trying to import data using excel file into my data model. I run all the steps but getting the error (please see attached screenshots). Apparently it is a warning but no error but I don't see any data and it says 0 rows imported.

Screenshots are attached .

Your help will be appreciated.

Thanks

entry.title: Re: Release Management with SAP Cloud ALM
entry.content:
I have a question here with respect to release management. Can the assigned release stop the import of transports in a feature if the release dates are in the future. How does the date range in release work, what is the significance of that.

-Srini

entry.title: Re: Odata $batch processing part 2
entry.content:
Hi Team,

In My scenario some set of data already exists in my custom table(with only two key fields and the same data is flowing inn as request in API) from SAP side.But the issue is if any duplicate records are there in the payload totoal request is failing with 401 Error.

Any suggestions here how to post the data if any duplicate present when compared with target custom table by skipping duplicate entry from each batch.

entry.title: Service Layer with return error code 408
entry.content:
Hi experts,

I am facing the issues with Service Layer when processing the data interface from SQL Data, we're built a portal to execute Job Service to POST data to Service Layer but unfortunately sometimes we got the http 408 code error return at around 23 hours same time.

Hope fully to get experiences sharing from experts.

Thanks so much,

entry.title: Re: Error while importing data in SAC model via excel file
entry.content: I think the error info clearly told you that there master doesn't exist. You need to mark the checkbox in import job setting to 'Upload local dimension with new members'.

entry.title: Re: how do I change the end date of the sale price in bulk
entry.content: Dear Customer, please use the APP: Manage Prices - Sales

entry.title: Re: Asset depreciation
entry.content:
Hello,

You can try the following: go to the app Asset BAlances and select the following 1. Key figure Group as "total depreciation (DEPR_TOTAL) and provide rest of the filter parameters as required (Company code, ledger etc.). Next keep only the G/L Account dimension in the row and Asset Class dimension in the column in remove al other filters. for better visibility select display property as ID only, you can also toggle the full screen. Next you can add any other dimension that you need. This gives a fair idea of depreciation G/L balances as per the asset class. 

Thank you!

entry.title: How to run TU door arrival with add-on. (EWM)
entry.content:
Nice to meet you.
I have a question about how to execute TU door arrival with an add-on.
I am trying to perform the door arrival operation at shipment using an add-on.
I tried Method:/SCWM/CL_SR_BO_TU->SET_TU_DOOR_DOCKING, but the door arrival state did not occur.

I created the add-on as follows.
Door assignment and checkpoint arrival have already been performed, and the door arrival part is now written.

1. Get door information with /SCWM/CL_SR_BO_TU->GET_TU_DOOR.

2. Set door arrival state with /SCWM/CL_SR_BO_TU->SET_TU_DOOR_DOCKING.
  The parameters are as follows.
  IV_ACTIVITY = 'SR09'
  IS_BO_TU_DOOR = obtained door information
  IV_NO_SYNCH_DLV = 'X'
  IV_REVERSAL = ' '
3. commit work

Please tell me how to do it.

entry.title: Do exist a field that show the Compensation exchange rate* amount USD
entry.content: Do we have a field in S4 hana that populate the compensation (payment) exchange rate * Amount USD?

entry.title: Re: Error while importing data in SAC model via excel file
entry.content: Are these dimensions public or private?

entry.title: Re: Error while importing data in SAC model via excel file
entry.content: These are public dimensions.

entry.title: Re: Error while importing data in SAC model via excel file
entry.content: Ok, for public diemension, you first need to maintain the master data before you can import.

entry.title: We are facing an Issue that Current, User can manually Change price at the sales order level without
entry.content:
We are facing an Issue that Current, User can manually Change price at the sales order level without approval. we need to implement an approval process to ensure that any price change are reviewed and authorized before the order is processed.

Requirements:

  1. When a user manually changes the price of an item on sales order, the system should trigger an approval request.
  2. The approval request should be sent to the designated approver for review.
  3. The approver should be able to approve or reject the price change 
  4. If approved the updated price should be reflected on the sales order
  5. If rejected, the original price should be reinstated, and user should be notified

This Our client Requirement please suggest a Solution.

entry.title: Modify SAP Advance Event Mesh Queue Message attributes like Message ID, Sequence ID etc...
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

 We have recently introduced SAP AEM in our SAP Landscape and I have a question regarding Publishing messages to a Queue. I could not get help from Standard SAP help documents or from Solace help Links. Any help on this would be really appreciated. We are on AEM Version 10.4.0.23

Every time we publish a message to Event Broker, either via Publishing directly to a Queue or via a TOPIC, AEM generates a Message ID for this. Along with this message id, AEM also store various other attributes of this message.

My Questions are:

1. Can we override AEM generated Message ID from our own Custom message ID (like IDOC number, Work Order number, or SCPI message ID etc...). In the attached screenshot it is: 984

2. How can we update other attributes of a Message like:   

Sequence Number, Message ID (0003dB), Replication Mate Msd ID

3. Why we have two Message IDs -  984 and 0003dB

Regards,

Anuj

entry.title: Required Sales Order Price Approval in sap S4 HANA Public Cloud
entry.content:
We are facing an Issue that Current, User can manually Change price at the sales order level without approval. we need to implement an approval process to ensure that any price change are reviewed and authorized before the order is processed.

Requirements:

  1. When a user manually changes the price of an item on sales order, the system should trigger an approval request.
  2. The approval request should be sent to the designated approver for review.
  3. The approver should be able to approve or reject the price change 
  4. If approved the updated price should be reflected on the sales order
  5. If rejected, the original price should be reinstated, and user should be notified

entry.title: Re: SAP Community - Where are you from?
entry.content: Hello, I am from Bangalore, India location. I am here to learn and share my learning with community members. I am also interested in expanding my networking with worldwide SAP experts. Looking forward for active participation in community discussions.

Hi team,

We want to know how frequently this fbl1n transaction access the archive server for retrieving the data

Arun George

entry.title: Re: Digital Skills Initiative Applicant Guide (For self-study candidates)
entry.content:
Hi @David_Chaviano,

I’m a female student from Pakistan currently involved in data analysis and was introduced to SAP by a colleague. I’m excited to advance my career in SAP data analysis. However, due to high USD conversion rates and financial constraints, pursuing certification is a challenge for me, as I am paying for my college through a job. I believe this initiative would be a great opportunity to overcome these barriers. I’ve created my SAP account and joined the beginner corner. Looking forward to your response!

entry.title: SAP Digital Skills Initiative
entry.content:
Hi @David_Chaviano,

I want to commend SAP for launching such a meaningful initiative! As a student from Pakistan already working in data analysis, I’m eager to dive into SAP data analysis. I first learned about SAP through a colleague, and since then, I've been excited about the opportunities in this field. However, with high USD conversion rates and financial challenges, getting certified has been difficult, especially since I’m paying for college through a job. This initiative is a game-changer for people like me, making SAP certification more accessible. I’ve created my SAP account and joined the beginner corner. I’m ready to begin my journey!

Best regards,

Javeria Feroz.

You can refer the below documentation regarding your requirement 

How to Set Up Approval Workflows in Your System 

Thank You

entry.title: OData Attachment Service: Line Item Attachment Failure
entry.content:
Hi,

I've recently implemented Attachment Service OData following this blog: [https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/odata-api-attachments-service-in-sap-s-4hana-cloud/bc-p/13521478/highlight/true]. While header-level attachments are working as expected, but I'm unable to attach files to line items.

I'm receiving a 201 response in both the gateway and Postman, indicating a successful operation, but the line item attachments are not being created.

Could you please suggest some troubleshooting steps or potential reasons for this issue?

scenario as below

Attaching object to FIdocument
BUS-BKPF and BSEG (tried with both for line item )
Linked SAP object key formed as

Header -M***1600****2024 (companycodedocnumyear) - success- attached- - response 201
LineItem - M***1600****2024901 ((companycodedocnumyearlienitenmun)) - failed to attach - but response 201

Can someone suggest on this if you have encountered similar issues.

 

Regards

Jakku

entry.title: Re: A Guide to Calling RFC Function Modules from SAP Integration Suite
entry.content:
Hi

Can you please elaborate  - the point you said as

"Once the RFC connection is created, add a new system connection to the subaccount using the RFC protocol."

Regards,

Venkat

entry.title: Access Customer Group and Period Volume Discounts in the database through the CCO plugin
entry.content:
Dear experts,

I hope this message finds you well.

I have a small requirement to change the discount procedure in customer checkout. For that, I have to access the discount percentages from the CCO database. I have tried several ways to access discount percentages; some do not work or match the requirements.

So, I want to know,

  1. How do I fetch Customer Group and Period Volume Discount Percentage from Squirrel DB for a Single Article/Material?

As an example, there is an Item called ‘GRO-000’ and I want to fetch Customer Group and Period and Volume Discount percentages separately.

Your valuable guidance, articles, or documents related to solving this problem is greatly appreciated.

Thank You.!

Best Regards,

Ashen.

entry.content:
no sufficient record found in control table

message:8A652

entry.title: Re: Customer field Not shown in ME21n and ME22N
entry.content: urgent

entry.title: Information of report S_ALR_87099930
entry.content:
Hello All,

I wanted some information about report S_ALR_87099930. 

Our client is looking for a report to compare two costing runs so that they can compare and get variance between BOM components for multiple finish products.

I checked the report and it gives the required data for one finish product at a time.

Can anybody let me know how to check variance for BOM components for two costing runs for multiple finish products.

Is there any other report to fulfill this requirement?

Thank you,

Sumant

entry.title: Re: Streamlining Time Sheet Approvals in SuccessFactors: Time Sheet Approval Center
entry.content:
Hi,

same here: permissions are enabled but it's not possible to access. 😞
Is there anything else besides the permissions that need to be adjusted?

Thanks!

Miriam

entry.title: Re: Financial Document (Letter of Credit) FTR_CREATE further processing in SAP S/4HANA Private Cloud
entry.content: Hello Experts, Could you please advise on this. Thank you, kind regards Thangaraj Thomas

entry.title: Transfer Forms to New Manager- SAP SuccessFactors PMGM- is this recommended?
entry.content:
**** Can "Transfer Forms to New Manager" tool be used when EC is implemented along with PMGM?******

**Form template settings has " Do Not Transfer Documents" and client HR is Manually changing  settings to  "Automatic Manager Transfer". - using the above tool to transfer forms and reverting back settings as of now ***

Dear Experts-

Our client has both PMGM and EC implemented and wants to transfer Performance forms to a new manager. However, they don't want to use the automatic function in HRIS sync for this (ie that forms are transferred automatically when you change an employee's manager in employee file\job information). The reason for this is that if an employee has started to fill out their form and the manager quits, the form ends up with the superior manager. Since the form may contain sensitive information, they don't want to use this feature because the employee may not want the superior manager to see this information.

 

They have found the tool “Transfer forms to new manager” and are happy with that. They activate settings in the form temporarily when they want to transfer a form, and then change back to "Do not send documents" again.

 

We know that the "Transfer forms to new manager" tool should not be used when Employee Central is enabled since the data is synced incorrectly. We could see that Org. Chart was updated with the new manager specified in the "Transfer form to new Manager" tool. But the old manager was still manager in the Position Org. Chart as well as in employee file. We assume this is an example of the data not syncing correctly. Could you please confirm this? And can you please provide other examples of what can be synced incorrectly?

 

Finally, we need a reply from you where you clearly state that this tool is not recommended when EC is enabled, if that is the case."

 

Below are the KBAs i referred-

2509058 - HRIS Sync: Why not to use Performance Management Document Transfer when Employee Central is enabled (sap.com)

entry.title: Synchronisation of processes between SAP Signavio and ServiceNow
entry.content:
Hi everybody,

We maintain our process landscape in Signavio and teams across our company consume it via Collaboration Hub. We are looking at synchronising processes (possibly multiple process levels) with one of the ServiceNow foundational tables, the Process table. Has anybody done that and if yes, how:

  1. which attributes are you synchronising?
  2. Are you:
    • using any of the reports available in Signavio Process Manager to export the data from Signavio, or
    • using the Dictionary Category Process and exporting the dictionary category, or
    • some other method?

I'm very interested in learning what approach you have chosen.
For context, in our company, Signavio is the single-source-of-truth for processes. The intention is to make the list of processes available to multiple teams, which use ServiceNow. This will lead to a more aligned company-wide view.

I look forward to hearing from you. Thanks.

Kind regards,

 

Vladimir

entry.title: Re: production order good receipts is done material is no...
entry.content:
Greetings,

it depends on many factors:

  • parameters for order confirmation
  • material master settings
  • overall confirmation scenario (header or per operation)

in the described scenario you can confirm activities in CO11N. You can also confirm quantities depending on OPK4 configuration and tolerance values. 

Best regards, Matjaž.

entry.title: Re: BYD2408 Remittance Advice form template sender selection
entry.content:
Hi one addition if you dont use the sender email via the output channel the application logic applies. Its not that always the default sender adress from tyhe Business config is automatically picked.

See i.e. Sales logic in KBA   2217871 - How is the Sender E-Mail Address of a Sales Document Determined? same exists for Logistic documents.

Form Template selection can be done hierarchically via the form template selection or on the account itself it can be "hardcoded"

Best regards Didi

entry.title: Re: Significance of WCM Objects
entry.content:
Greetings,

I think you should familiarize yourself with official SAP Help which is sometimes a bit difficult to understand so I would suggest the following additional resources:

https://www.slideshare.net/slideshow/sap-pm-wcm-enhanced-model-entire-process-flow-with-sap-screenshots/14696362

https://www.wcm-it.com/english/product/processes/

Best regards, Matjaž.

entry.title: Re: GRC Tuesdays: Intelligent Screening with SAP Watch List Screening
entry.content: This is a very nice blog, thanks a lot for sharing the information.

entry.title: Re: Asset depreciation
entry.content: Well, I am not sure about the exact T-code for this app, as it has not been mentioned in Apps Library. But you may try S_ALR_87011964. It may not be as apt/accurate as the Fiori app suggested above.

entry.title: Re: SAC Script has got no IF (... OR ...) ?
entry.content:
Hi, I have a similar problem, I want to condition the second input control from the first imput control, but for some reazon is not working

 

var input_control = InputControl_Cta.getInputControlDataSource().getActiveSelectedMembers();
for ( var i=0; i < input_control.length; i++)
{
 
if( ( input_control[i].id === "F62010030") || ( input_control[i].id === "F62010032") || ( input_control[i].id === "F62010031") )
 
{
 
var canal1 =["15"];
InputControl_Canal.getInputControlDataSource().setSelectedMembers(canal1);
}
 
if( ( input_control[i].id === "F62010031") ) 
 
{
 
var canal2 =["13"];
InputControl_Canal.getInputControlDataSource().setSelectedMembers(canal2);
}
}

entry.title: Diminishing/Declining Method for Chart of Depreciation IFRS
entry.content:
Good day,

The business has activated ledger IFRS and according to IFRS.org the following depreciation methods are supported - straight-line method, the diminishing balance method and the
units of production method. However, we cannot find any depreciation keys that supports the diminishing balance method assigned to the Chart of Depreciation IFRS. We do see the declining method as part of Chart of Depreciation 3010.

As per IFRS.org (link) the business requires the ability to assign the diminishing/declining method to the IFRS Chart of Depreciation/Depreciation Area as well, not just the Local AU Chart of Depreciation 3010.

Kind regards,

Andro

entry.title: how to Send Hyperlink of Fiori App in Workflow Task
entry.content:
Dear All,

 We have an requirement to open Fiori app from workflow task.

we need to send Fiori App link in workflow task and when user receives work item in Fiori Inbox , user should able to click on Link Of Fiori Link and app will open with corresponding data ex- PO.

Please help if any one has done similar development.

Thanks,

Arpita

entry.title: Re: Transporting content in SAP Build Apps (SAP Cloud Transport Management integration)
entry.content: Hi @marihirvi, thx for the blog. Do we need to create 4 sub accounts (dev,test,prod,central) tenants? or can we put central in dev subaccount?

entry.title: Re: Digital Skills Initiative Applicant Guide (For self-study candidates)
entry.content:
Hi David,

My name is Nguyet.

I am a woman and part of an ethnic minority in Vietnam. After working for six years in two Japanese manufacturing companies, I had the opportunity to learn a lot about production processes and supply chains, as well as gaining exposure to various ERP systems. I am truly fascinated by how these systems operate to ensure optimized workflows and enable company leadership to monitor business performance.

That’s why I transitioned to a new role as an SAP consultant this July. I am still in the learning phase and plan to dive deeper into the PP module. I came across information that SAP offers free exam vouchers for the SAP community, and I believe this would be extremely beneficial to me at this stage.

I am truly grateful that you are offering this support to others, and I would really appreciate your assistance. It would be a great honor for me and a valuable opportunity to grow in my career. Thank you for considering my application for the free certification attempt.

All the best,

Nguyet

entry.title: NPM_CONFIG_LOGLEVEL=error (STDOUT, STG)# in cloud application programming model in SAP BTP
entry.content:
Hi,

I created CAP project and when I am deploying and getting Error  and please find the below error log

#2.0#2024 09 30 03:19:47.338#Z#DEBUG#com.sap.cloud.lm.sl.xs2.c66d594c-7eda-11ef-9be6-eeee0a9c20dd.MyHanaApp-db-deployer.executeTaskTask#
######org.cloudfoundry.multiapps.controller.persistence.services.ProcessLoggerProvider########flowable-async-job-executor-thread-4307###
[PollExecuteTaskStatusExecution] [MyHanaApp-db-deployer] 685138a8-0b42-41a9-a999-499c1d9380d4 [2024-09-30T02:51:32.794910] NPM_CONFIG_LOGLEVEL=error (STDOUT, STG)#
 
Please find the attatched screen shot
 
After looking the error i had done the below but still i am getting error 
 
npm install @sap/hdi-deploy
npm install -g npm@10.8.3
npm install @sap/hana-client

entry.title: Re: S4HC Supplier Operational Evaluation Score Calculation Examples
entry.content:
Hi Qi Liu,

I tried but the inspection lot scores (UD has been done) did not show in the Supplier Evaluation by Quality app and the Operational Supplier Evaluation app.

And the Operational Supplier Evaluation app calulated the overall scores by average of relevant scores (not using the weighting ).

Could you please help out the issues ?

entry.title: Re: 🟡 Petoberfest 2024
entry.content: #petoberfest #devtoberfest

entry.title: Re: Sales Invoices for Export sales with payment of IGST...
entry.content:
Hi @Suryaprakash1 ,

Thanks for the reply, really appreciate it.

Actually, even if it is the same export customer we need a checkbox or a control to apply IGST. Overall, it is two types of cases one wherein we need to apply IGST and one where we don't even if it is the same customer.

As an Example,

Case 1: Customer ABCD and IGST Applied and calculated.

Case 2: Customer ABCD and IGST Not applied and calculated.

 

Thanks for all the help,

Bhavesh

entry.title: Linking dynamic excel sheet with SAP
entry.content: I wish to link dynamic excel sheet with RFQ portal as I need to enter name of each vendor manually and can't paste all the names together due to constraints of SAP. is there any way that i can enter all names or vendor code together

entry.title: Re: Where i can document list with all its uses cases

I am looking for detailed information on the entries generated by each program, especially when multiple programs are running in the cloud

entry.title: Fiori Launchpad does not recognize annotation file
entry.content:
When I test my application on my local machine it works correctly but when I deploy it on the fiori launchpad in btp it seems that it does not recognize my annotations file MM_PUR_PR_PROFNL_MAINTA_ANNO_MDL, does anyone know why this could be happening?

I really appreciate your support
Regards

Annotations

 Manifest

entry.title: Re: Where i can document list with all its uses cases
entry.content: Please specify the detailed scenario. There is no general document.

Hello,

entry.title: Re: Variant Table - Line not valid
entry.content:
Hello Conrad,

This helps to some extent. 
I also work in SCE mode, where we do not create configuration profile for the KMAT and we have to access the variant table using CU60.

Thanks!

entry.title: Re: MD01 MRP Run should create only one PR for multiple m...
entry.content:
Hello @raman_ibm 

I think you approach the problem from a wrong angle. You don't really need a single purchase requisition if materials are source from the same contract. What you need is to convert multiple purchase requisitions into a single purchase order.

Besides what you require is not possible with standard MRP logic, as MRP plans on material level, not on a contract level.

Best regards

Dominik Tylczynski

entry.title: Re: how to add a field in ALV of transaction IW47
entry.content: There are two solutions to add a custom field to IW47 ALV, EITHER 1) create an append on DDIC structure RIHAFVR, OR 2) enhance OBJECT_TAB + enhance G_FIELDCAT_TAB. Restart the program: /NIW47 or /NSE38 RIAFRU20.

entry.title: Returning Excel with a CAP service
entry.content:
Hello, I am trying this feature in order to download a dynamically generated excel from a UI5 app: https://cap.cloud.sap/docs/node.js/best-practices#custom-streaming-beta

The way this:

const readable = Readable.from(buffer)
return {
  value: readable,
  $mediaContentType: 'application/vnd.openxmlformats-officedocument.spreadsheetml.sheet',
  $mediaContentDispositionFilename: `${fileName}.xlsx`,
}

Where readable is of type stream.Readable and the buffer comes from ExcelJS.

When I trigger it in the browser, it correctly downloads an attachment of type XLSX, but the content is:

{
@odata.context: "$metadata#Edm.Binary"
value: {
  value: "[object Readable]"
  $mediaContentType: "application/vnd.openxmlformats-officedocument.spreadsheetml.sheet"
  $mediaContentDispositionFilename: "DCT - 212 - TEST TAX - SSD 2024-10-05.xlsx"
  }
}

So somehow, the readable stream it not used correctly down the line. Am I doing something wrong?

entry.title: Re: I created a little helper UI5 App to check if you received your badges
entry.content:
@priyankaG @thomas_jung 
Yes, i just updated it, just input the same as on the gameboard:

https://devtoberfest.marianzeis.de/

entry.title: Re: BNJ and BLD test scripts are not supported in test au...
entry.content:
Hi @Amin_S ,

This is currently not part of the standard content which is delivered in the tool.
You can find the complete list of supported automates here:  Automated Test Scripts Index

Regards
Anurag

entry.title: Re: SAP Enable Now for Fieldglass
entry.content: Than you Shane, I will forward your response to my colleague whi is working on this request.

entry.title: Re: Where i can document list with all its uses cases
entry.content:
Fir instance  document types generated when running the Advanced Foreign Currency Valuation program. Could you provide a detailed list of the document types that are created through this process for example 
like wise we have multiple programmed in system that are running and for each such programs document get generated so would like to know such exhaustive list that can be beneficial to business users

entry.title: Credit checks available in SAP Credit Management (FSCM)
entry.content:
As SAP Credit Management (FSCM) is a different solution, several differences can be noticed that are impacting how credit checks function. Rather than providing a comprehensive guide to credit management, this blog is focusing on credit checks only. This blog explores the differences compared to ECC classic credit management (SD-BF-CM/FI-AR-CR) such as configuration in transaction OVA8 and on Credit Account (Business Partner) level, describes the presently available credit checks and mentions enhancement options.

Disclaimer: The information is relevant for SAP S/4HANA Private Cloud (On-Premise) releases, specifically the processing of SD Sales documents. The blog points out the standard design and only gives general suggestions to adjust the functionality, any modifications need to be implemented and tested at your own responsibility.

Content

  1. General remarks on credit checks
  2. SAP Credit Management overall configuration
  3. Available credit check steps
  4. Credit check enhancements

General remarks on credit checks

  • Credit check from SD side is only performed for Sales Orders (activated in transaction OVAK) or Deliveries (activated in transaction OVAD, at the Delivery/PGI level). Billing documents do not undergo credit check.
  • In transaction OVAK, the credit check type supported is 'Automatic' (Type 'D'). Simple credit checks are no longer recommended or supported by SAP.
  • The credit group is maintained in transactions OVAK or OVAD. In standard 01 for sales orders, 02 for delivery, 03 for PGI level.

  • Credit check can be triggered on a sales document if the Credit Control Area (VBAK/LIKP-KKBER) and Credit Account - based on Payer - (VBAK/LIKP-KNKLI) are determined. From the Credit Account the system is reading the risk class (VBAK/LIKP-CTLPC) which are used to look up the configuration in OVA8 transaction.
  • No credit check can be triggered on delivery without reference to sales order (e.g. STO) as the necessary master data is missing.
  • In transaction OVA8 the SAP Credit Management check has to be flagged, Reaction and Status/Block setting maintained per requirement. This is needed to even initiate a credit check from SD side. The credit checks are executed then on FSCM side per check rule.
  • During the saving of a sales document, credit checks are being executed. Even if the user is only making changes to a non-critical field (such as customer reference, material description, or header text) without increasing the quantity or credit price, a credit check process will still be started.
  • The credit check is valid for the whole sales document, the credit status is a header level field. The credit block cannot be set per items and credit release cannot be done for specific items either.
  • In OVA8 transaction the ‘Item check’ flag can be set to trigger a credit check already when items are being added to the sales order. This informs the user if the order would fail on the credit check so they can decide to abort the document processing.
  • The credit active indicator (item category setting, VBAP/LIPS-CMPNT) is not directly controlling the credit check, but the credit value of sales document items.
  • The following 'Overall Credit Status' results can occur:
    • 'Approved' - CMGST = 'A': All the assigned credit checks are OK, credit block did not occur. Sales order or delivery can be further processed. In general, the document will be updated to the credit exposure.
    • 'Blocked / Not Approved' - CMGST = 'B': The document was credit blocked because one of the credit checks failed or a check which also uses credit status field failed. In general, blocked sales orders don't update the exposure, but deliveries do update even if credit blocked.
    • 'Released' - CMGST = 'D': The document is getting released manually by the responsible person (credit analyst, credit representative). Releasing a sales document does not happen automatically (manual DCD release or VKM* transaction). During the release, fields VBAK/LIKP-CMFRE and VBAK/LIKP-AMTBL are also getting populated. The individual credit statuses are not changed during release to keep a record of failed credit check.
    • 'Not Performed / Not checked' - CMGST = ' ': The sales document did not get its credit status filled because the prerequisites to perform credit check are not met or setting the status is not configured or a No check routine was bypassing the credit check.
  • The FSCM credit statuses are as below:
    • VBAK/LIKP-CMPS_CM = Status of Credit Check SAP Credit Management
    • VBAK/LIKP-CMPS_TE = Status of Technical Error SAP Credit Management
  • Overall credit status is based on the credit check status. Also, the Payment Cards authorization (VBAK/LIKP-CMPSK) and Letter of Credit (Financial documents, VBAK/LIKP-CMPSI) check can set the overall credit status to blocked if these failed.

References:
KBA 2788718 - Configuration checklist for SAP Credit Management (FSCM)
KBA 3196063 - Credit check results and credit status of a sales document
KBA 2786413 - Role of 'Credit active' indicator in credit management

SAP Credit Management overall configuration

  • Credit master data is defined on Business Partner with role UKM000. Settings are maintained in transaction UKM_BP (preselected for role UKM000) on 'General Data' -> 'Credit profile' tab (important: Risk class and Check rule) and on 'Credit Segment data' tab (important: Credit limit, Option to simulate credit check).
  • Credit segment is the organizational unit of FSCM. Credit segments need to be created and assigned to the equivalent credit control area. On the sales documents the system is determining the credit control area (CCAr) the same as on ECC (userexit or payer master data or based on sales area or based on company code) and this value is mapped to the credit segment.

  • The credit checks are assigned to a credit 'Check Rule'. Follow SPRO > FSCM > ... > Credit Limit Check > Define Checking Rules. There are SAP delivered check rules, but any custom credit check rule can be created and credit check steps assigned per business requirement.
  • The ‘Check Rule’ is maintained on the Business Partner -> Credit Profile tab in UKM_BP transaction. Accordingly, the credit checks added to the check rule are relevant for all credit segments of the Business Partner.
  • OVA8 transaction looks different, the individual credit checks are no longer listed here. The only available credit check is SAP Credit Management. There is only one 'Reaction' and 'Status/Block' setting that can be maintained for the overall SAP Credit Management check. This is the correct system design, because the individual credit check step results (which are assigned to a given check rule) are also aggregated.
  • The seasonal factor settings in OVA8 have also been removed. An alternative is the Business Partner/Credit segment level Further Information options.
  • With FSCM it is possible to execute the credit check on main segment only, subsegment only or both main segment and subsegment levels. Follow SPRO > FSCM > ... > Master Data > Create Credit Segments. From S/4HANA 1909 release it is possible to decide between these options when maintaining the subsegment. (On previous releases the ‘Add. Contribution to Main Segment’ setting also triggered main segment level credit check.)
  • The previously known credit account hierarchy or parent-child credit account setup is still possible with FSCM. The Business Partners can be assigned to each other via Relationships. FUKM001 category is used for the parent/higher-level/superordinate credit account and TUKM001 category is used for the child/lower-level/subordinate credit account. From the sales order the child account is triggering the credit check and on FSCM side the system reads the details of the relationship. To have a working setup the child account should have a different check rule, without credit limit check steps (010 Static, 030 Dynamic) assigned. The DCD is created for the child account.
  • Credit check can be executed on sales orders even if credit master data is not maintained on the Business Partner. As SD side triggers the credit check, FSCM side creates the necessary master data from default settings - given that default check rule, risk class determination are maintained.

References:
KBA 2788718 - Configuration checklist for SAP Credit Management (FSCM)
KBA 2207394 - FSCM less credit checks in OVA8
KBA 2761313 - Maintain Credit Checks for SAP Credit Management (FSCM)
KBA 2818007 - Credit limit seasonal factor in FSCM Credit Management
SAP Note 2270544 - S4TWL - Credit Management
KBA 2801882 - Credit limit check with main segment and/or subsegment in SAP Credit Management (FSCM)
KBA 2706299 - Parent-child relationship of business partners for credit check in SAP Credit Management (FSCM)
KBA 3017134 - Automatic creation of credit segment

Available credit check steps

The following credit check steps are available in FSCM and can be assigned to check rules. Certain credit check steps can have settings maintained on credit segment level. If there is no segment level setting for a given credit segment then the overall settings per Check Step Level are valid.

010 Static Check of Credit Exposure (UKM_CHECK_010)

  • The credit check is comparing the credit limit to the aggregated credit value of all exposure types (open orders, deliveries, billing documents, open items) + the credit value of the sales document being created.
  • Static credit check ‘open orders’/’open deliveries’ exclusion flag no longer available, the whole credit exposure is used during the credit check.
  • It is not advised to add both Static and Dynamic credit limit checks to the same check rule.

020 Check for Maximum Document Value (UKM_CHECK_020) 

  • The sales order or delivery value should not exceed the maximum value that is specified per credit segment. The value is stored in the currency of the credit segment.
  • This type of credit check makes sense, for example, if you process orders for new customers whose credit limits have not yet been defined.
  • The standard check step is not designed for a 0 (zero) value check.

030 Dynamic Limit Check with Credit Horizon (UKM_CHECK_030) 

  • If the credit exposure within the horizon is exceeding the credit limit the document is credit blocked.
  • Only the open sales order items with delivery-related billing belong to the dynamic part based on the material availability date (VBEP-MBDAT). The sales documents with order-related billing are completely included in the dynamic credit limit check. The open deliveries, billing document and open items also belong to the static part.
  • With FSCM the credit horizon is set in days and the end of the credit horizon is a specific date. 0 (zero) days are not advised as credit horizon, in that case from the dynamic part only the credit values up to the current date are used.
  • Even the sales orders outside the credit horizon are credit checked and if the limit is already exceeded the credit status will be blocked.

100 Check for Maximum Dunning Level (UKM_CHECK_100)

  • In this credit check you specify the highest dunning level you want to allow. The dunning level is tracked and stored in the credit data in the customer master record.

110 Check for Age of Oldest Open Item (UKM_CHECK_110)

  • This check refers to the 'Vector' data, transferred from FI side with the UKM_TRANSFER_VECTOR report. The oldest open item must not be older than the number of days specified.

120 Check of Payment Behavior Index (UKM_CHECK_120) 

  • Credit check against Key figure DSO (= Days Sales Outstanding) specified. The open items may not be open for a longer period than specified as DSO (days sales outstanding).

Good to know

  • The steps 100, 110, and 120 relate to key figures located in the credit segment data of a Business Partner. You find the key figures on the Payment Behavior Key Figures tab of UKM_BP, Credit Segment Data.
  • The key figures on this tab are written by program UKM_TRANSFER_VECTOR. This program needs to be run regularly so that the key figures in the credit segment data are up to date, thereby enabling the above credit checks to be performed correctly.

130 Check for Overdue Open Items (UKM_CHECK_130) 

  • This step is related to the TIMESTAMP in the FSCM with which the documents are stored and written, mainly set with the ‘posting’ or ‘document’ date of the related items. The proportion of overdue open items (that exceed the specified number of days) in the total of open items should not exceed the percentage specified.
  • SPRO > ... > Credit Exposure Update > Define Liability Categories >> Grid flag is needed so the relevant items flow into the evaluation.
  • The percentage and day in credit segment level should be defined, otherwise the credit check result will be incorrect and just display as 0% (‘ ‘ days).

140 Check for Credit Limit Validity (UKM_CHECK_140) 

  • Checks if the credit limit is still valid according to the validity date maintained on Credit Segment level of Business Partner.
  • The new check step was introduced with S/4HANA On-Premise/Private Cloud 2022. (S4CORE 107)
  • On lower releases the credit limit is still used in credit checks, even if it has already expired.

Removed checks

  • Credit check for ‘Critical fields’ is removed.
  • The ‘Next review date’ check does not exist in previous form.
  • The ‘User 1’, ‘User 2’, ‘User 3’ checks from OVA8 transaction are not directly available.

Logging

If any of the credit check steps assigned to the check rule fail, then the SAP Credit Management check fails and VBAK/LIKP-CMPS_CM will be set to ‘B’. To check which individual credit check step failed the below options exist:

  • Log: Credit check details logging can be activated on the 'Check Rule' level, it is suggested to use the extensive logging. With active logging the system stores the checked credit value, credit limit, credit exposure, payment behavior key figures, maximum document, etc. The log can be checked from transaction UKM_LOGS_DISPLAY.
  • Reason for blocked overall status: Transaction UKM_CASE/SCASE/UKM_MY_DCDS list the 'Documented Credit Decisions' created for sales documents. In the layout, columns showing which credit check step failed (e.g. column Static Check, Maximum Doc. Value Check, etc.) can be added. VKM* transactions can only show that 'SAP Credit Management' is causing the credit blocked status.

References:
KBA 2761313 - Maintain Credit Checks for SAP Credit Management (FSCM)
SAP Note 2561725 - Simplified functions in SAP Credit Management in SAP S/4HANA
KBA 2926009 - Credit check step per segment customizing in SAP Credit Management (FSCM)
KBA 3196063 - Credit check results and credit status of a sales document
KBA 2939371 - Dynamic Limit Check with Credit Horizon in SAP Credit Management (FSCM)

Credit check enhancements

Userexit credit checks

  • Customized credit check steps can be created by implementing credit check steps in BAdI UKM_CHECK_STEP. Copying an existing credit check step and modifying it is advised. The BAdI can access data via the input parameter IO_ACCOUNT of class CL_UKM_ACCOUNT.
  • The credit check step coding is executed on FSCM side, if more SD data is needed consider a custom implementation of BADI_SD_CM (Methods FSCM_CREDIT_CHECK_ORDER, FSCM_CREDIT_CHECK_DELVRY) or direct modification on FMs SD_ORDER_CREDIT_CHECK, SD_DELIVERY_CREDIT_CHECK.
  • The custom credit check steps should no longer refer to S066, S067 and KNKK data.
  • An alternative to ‘Critical Fields’ credit check could only be achieved by custom credit check logic.

References:
KBA 3427949 - FSCM alternative for User 1, User 2, User 3 credit checks
KBA 3454258 - Critical fields credit check on S/4HANA - FSCM

Credit check based on requested quantity

  • This functionality is not available in the standard. The static and dynamic credit check calculates the credit value of the order being created by using the confirmed quantity. You could create a custom credit check step to modify the standard Static/Dynamic credit check. Alternatively, the credit value (LV_AMOUNT) sent from SD to FSCM in BADI_SD_CM -> Method FSCM_CREDIT_CHECK_ORDER could be adjusted by custom logic.

References:
KBA 3276107 - Credit check with order/requested quantity instead of confirmed quantity (FSCM)

Exclude values from Static credit check

  • Custom credit check could be implemented based on the 010 check step, where you exclude values from the credit exposure that the system is pulling. Alternatively, if orders are to be generally excluded, then update group 15 could be used (Credit Control Area setting, transaction OB45). Or based on the requirement the liability categories could be switched to ‘Statistical’ (SPRO > ... > Credit Exposure Update > Define Liability Categories). Keep in mind that the configuration changes have a wider impact, not only relevant for one credit check step/credit segment.

References:
KBA 3460701 - Static credit check control flags not available with FSCM

Avoid credit checks (1) - Released documents are still unchecked

  • When a sales order was already released further credit checks can be avoided. The release validity can be extended by OVA8 settings of ‘Deviation%’ and ‘Number of days’.
  • To avoid the credit check during saving the following criteria has to be fulfilled: released credit value (VBAK/LIKP-AMTBL) is more or equal than momentary credit value AND release date (VBAK/LIKP-CMFRE) is valid on the day of new credit check
  • Frequent cause for recheck after release: released credit value is not the full credit value of the sales document (e.g. ATP can’t confirm all items, PR not created due to missing data).
  • If an order is released and delivered on the same day, the delivery is not rechecked again (LIKP-CMGST = 'D').
  • On the day of release there is no new credit check (behavior can be changed by modification of FMs SD_ORDER_CREDIT_CHECK / SD_DELIVERY_CREDIT_CHECK, validity date check GT instead of GE)

References:
SAP Note 718260 - Released documents are still unchecked

Avoid credit checks (2) - Routine to bypass FSCM credit check

  • The credit check can be deactivated through custom routine in OVA8, field ‘No credit check’.
  • Do not directly use the 001, 002 routines, those are only examples.
  • BYPASS-CREDIT_MANAGEMENT variable is to be set to true ('X') to skip the SAP Credit Management check.
  • STATUS_RESET-CREDIT_MANAGEMENT variable is to be set to true ('X') to clear/reset the SAP Credit Management status (VBAK/LIKP-CMPS_CM).
  • The routine runs during any credit check (except while credit release is still valid).
  • If you skip the credit check (and there was previously also no credit check executed), then the overall credit status will be ‘Not performed’.
  • The STATUS_RESET parameter is needed to clear the previous failed credit status (VBAK/LIKP-CMPS_CM), but it results in overall credit status as ‘Not performed’ (VBAK/LIKP-CMGST).

References:
KBA 2469457 - Bypass credit check in custom routine in SAP Credit Management (FSCM)

Credit check message details

  • Reactions ‘C’ and ‘D’ have been deprecated. Warning messages about failed credit check appear as a pop-up showing the details. Error messages are displayed only as one-line text (VM010) informing the user that the credit check failed.
  • The pop-up message and the error message could be modified in own BADI_SD_CM implementation by adjusting method FSCM_DISPLAY_CREDIT_MESSAGES. Variable I_MESSAGE_TYPE controls the reaction type.

References:
KBA 2871140 - Credit check result details not shown with error reaction - FSCM

The current blog discusses many useful information to setup credit checks with FSCM. Review the requirements with the business in detail and consider the described standard design. Before switching to SAP Credit Management (FSCM) consider the shared information in detail to be able to move from ECC classic credit management with the least effort. Keep in mind that modifications and own check steps are considered as custom code, hence you are required to implement and test in detail. 
For further information feel free to ask questions on SAP Community.

entry.title: What’s New for SAP S/4HANA Cloud: Leave Us Your Feedback!
entry.content:
We want to get your feedback on the What’s New for SAP S/4HANA Cloud (available here), so that we can make the information we provide even more useful for you. To do this, we are making a new commenting feature available.

By clicking on “Leave a comment”, you can leave your direct feedback for the What’s New document authors. They can then evaluate your feedback and incorporate it into the documentation, if needed. We hope that by opening this channel of communication, we can learn how to further improve our What’s New content for you. For this, your feedback is essential! 

To use this feature, you need to be logged into the Help Portal. The feature is only available in the English version of the documents. To learn more about how the feedback process works in SAP Help Portal, see Giving Documentation Feedback.

entry.title: Getting Started with Edge Integration Cell on AWS: A Setup Guide Using SAP Integration Suite
entry.content:
What is Edge Integration Cell?

Edge Integration Cell (EIC), part of the SAP Integration Suite, brings a powerful hybrid integration runtime that bridges the gap between the cloud and your private landscape. It provides the flexibility to seamlessly manage APIs and run integration scenarios within a controlled environment, combining the best of both worlds.

With its hybrid deployment model, EIC makes it easy to design and monitor integration content in the cloud, while still allowing it to be securely deployed and run in a customer managed private environment.

 

Use cases and Advantages of EIC

  • Security or compliance use cases: Sensitive data stays safely within an enterprise's firewall, following strict rules to keep it within a private environment.
  • Migration path for SAP Process Integration customers: EIC helps customers design and monitor integration content in the cloud, while keeping deployment and execution fully within their private environments.

This blog offers a step-by-step guide to deploying Edge Integration Cell on AWS, with two setup options: a quick setup for trying out the simplest setup and a high-availability configuration for production. Whether you're looking for a quick test or a scalable, production-ready deployment, this guide makes it easy to configure SAP BTP and AWS resources. Once the setup is complete, the SAP Integration Suite can deploy integration flows securely to on-premise setup using EIC. 

 

Architecture

Both the quick setup and the high availability (HA) setup architecture for deploying EIC on AWS utilize Amazon Elastic Kubernetes Service (EKS). The quick setup uses the default Virtual Private Cloud (VPC) in the region, with all storage handled by Amazon Elastic Block Store (EBS) and smaller compute and storage resources for a simple and efficient deployment. In contrast, the HA architecture is spread across at least three availability zones for redundancy, with dynamic storage managed by a combination of EBS and Amazon Elastic File System (EFS), along with additional services like Amazon Relational Database Service (RDS) for persistence and Redis for caching.

On the SAP BTP side, the Edge Integration Cell must be activated within the SAP Integration Suite. Once the EIC is configured and successfully deployed on the AWS EKS cluster, integration flows can be designed and seamlessly deployed to the new EIC runtime, combining the powerful integration capabilities of SAP Integration Suite with the customer managed resources on AWS.

 

Edge Integration Cell setup overview on SAP BTP and AWS

Here is an outline of the steps involved in setting up and deploying Edge Integration Cell from SAP BTP environment to AWS.

Setup on AWS:

 

Setup on SAP BTP: 

 

You can find the detailed setup instructions in the following GitHub repository – Deploy SAP Integration Suite - Edge Integration Cell on Amazon Web Services.

 

Additional resources

Here are some helpful resources related to Edge Integration Cell:

  • What is Edge Integration Cell | SAP Help
  • Deploy Edge Integration Cell on Microsoft Azure using Azure Kubernetes Service
  • Deploy Edge Integration Cell on a custom server using SUSE Rancher
  • FAQ on Edge Integration Cell
  • SAP Integration Suite | SAP Help

 

Conclusion

I hope this blog has helped you set up Edge Integration Cell on AWS, making it easier to connect SAP on-premise solutions with the flexibility to design and monitor integrations in the cloud. By following these steps, you can build secure, high-performing business applications that blend innovation with control in a safe environment.

I want to express my gratitude to our SAP team members, Weikun Liu, Sangeetha Krishnamoorthy, Dante Alipio Jr., and Sivakumar N for their valuable contributions, guidance, and support. If you have any questions, feel free to contact paa@sap.com.

entry.title: Integrate SAP LeanIX with Integration Assessment in SAP Integration Suite
entry.content:
Integration Assessment

In the mission, you will start using Integration Assessment for defining, documenting, and executing an integration technology strategy for your organization. After you will have configured Integration Assessment you will process a sample business solution request to specify your integration requirements and an interface request to determine the most suitable integration technology with the help of the intelligent proposal service.
 

SAP LeanIX

Key features of LeanIX are:

  1. Application Portfolio Management: Helps assess and manage the applications in use, facilitating retirement or replacement decisions.
  2. Integration with SAP: Provides tailored functionalities for SAP users, allowing them to leverage existing SAP data and processes.
  3. Collaboration Tools: Supports teamwork and communication among stakeholders involved in enterprise architecture initiatives.
  4. Reporting and Dashboards: Offers visualizations and analytics to track progress and performance metrics.

SAP system integration is a key process for enterprise architects embarking on application modernization. Yet, it isn’t always straightforward.

SAP is the world’s largest provider of enterprise application software, used by 87% of all global commerce and 99 of the world’s 100 largest companies. At the core of SAP’s impressive market share is their ability to deliver value through seamless integration. Yet, to benefit from this, SAP must be connected to other key business systems, which may be in need of re-organization.

The technical and strategic change management considerations related to SAP system integration require thorough preparation. Many companies fail to meet the full potential of integration because of these challenges.

In this guide, we’ll break down seven steps you can take to meet integration challenges head-on and execute the process with confidence.

 

SAP Standard Integration Package by NTT DATA Business Solutions

This integration package is designed to synchronize data between SAP LeanIX and Integration Assessment, focusing primarily on the synchronization of Applications, Vendors and Interface Requests. Utilizing the SAP Integration Suite, this synchronization process ensures a seamless, secure, and up-to-date data flow. This enhances the accuracy and efficiency of IT integration strategies, providing comprehensive insights for integration assessments

Note: Due to the equivalence of 'Vendors' in Integration Assessment to 'Providers' in SAP LeanIX for simplicity and clarity, 'vendor' is used as the uniform term throughout the package.

The package comprises four distinct integrations, each serving unique functions and designed to operate independently. Users can select and deploy any iFlow according to their specific requirements and needs:

  1. Fullsync Integration Assessment Vendors: Synchronizes all vendors (providers) from SAP LeanIX to Integration Assessment. This interface is triggered manually and operates once per trigger.
  2. Fullsync Integration Assessment Applications: Synchronizes all applications from SAP LeanIX to Integration Assessment. This includes optional synchronization of application instances (on-premise/public-cloud/private-cloud). This interface is also manually triggered and operates once per trigger.
  3. Create Integration Assessment Vendor: Activated whenever a factsheet of type 'provider' is created in SAP LeanIX. This event triggers the creation of a new vendor in Integration Assessment, ensuring real-time data updates.
  4. Create Integration Assessment Application: Similar to the vendor creation interface, this is activated when a factsheet of type 'application' is created in SAP LeanIX. It triggers the creation of a new application in Integration Assessment, including the synchronization of application instances (on-premise/public-cloud/private-cloud) if applicable. This interface also requires manual triggering and operates once per trigger.
  5. Create SAP LeanIX Interface Factsheet: Synchroniz a SAP LeanIX Interface Fact Sheet from Integration Assessment Interface Requests at a configurable interval. Applications selected in the Interface Request will also be referenced in the Fact Sheet, provided they have been synced by this package.

https://api.sap.com/package/SAPLeanIXIntegrationwithSAPIntegrationSuite/overview 

User Journey

New created applications in SAP LeanIX

Automated Sync to Integration Assessment

New Business Solution Request in Integration Assessment

New Interface Fact Sheet in SAP LeanIX

Outlook

  • Does that Integration help you?
  • Would you like to integrate with other tools?
  • What are you use cases?

Please use the comment or send me a message 🙂
I'll be happy to discuss your scenario and requirements around SAP Integration Suite and SAP LeanIX!

entry.title: Benz
Managing Test Cases and Defects: A Comprehensive Guide
entry.content:
Efficient management of test cases and defects is essential for ensuring software quality and streamlining the QA process. Proper handling of both elements enhances collaboration, reduces delays, and helps deliver a more stable product.

  1. Organizing Test Cases: Start by creating a structured test case repository. Group test cases by functionality, modules, or features to ensure easy navigation. Each test case should be well-documented, including clear objectives, steps, expected outcomes, and priority levels. This ensures consistency and improves test coverage.

  2. Traceability and Test Mapping: Ensure that each test case is mapped to specific requirements. Traceability helps teams identify gaps in testing, ensuring that all functionalities are covered. It also assists in tracking how defects relate to specific test cases and requirements, improving communication between development and QA teams.

  3. Defect Life Cycle Management: Implement a streamlined process for managing defects. This includes identifying, logging, prioritizing, and assigning defects for resolution. Detailed defect reporting with clear descriptions and severity levels helps developers understand and fix issues faster.

  4. Automation Integration: Integrate automation to manage repetitive tasks like regression tests. Automating test case execution and defect tracking increases efficiency and reduces human errors.

By adopting structured approaches and using test management tools, teams can handle test cases and defects more efficiently, leading to better software quality and faster releases.

entry.title: The Sun Isn't Falling The Horizon Is Moving Up
entry.content:
The Beatles' line about "the eyes in her head see the world spinning round" in that sunrise and sunset are as much from your earthly perspective as your cosmic one applies to the coming and going of discussion forums.

As I started this post I saw I appear to have 2 other posts in draft state in this Welcome Corner space, except they also show as published and are quite old (one platform removed). And a recent draft I completed to the point of sending for approval when simultaneously that space was being frozen from new content. Augh, as Charley Brown always said when hit with a block unexpectedly yet also repeatedly.

Published yet drafts

My Spock logic take is these were degraded in state during the last platform switch, as I am pretty sure my  central blog control had no old drafts stuck. Occasionally I'll see this in other topic areas, including phantom comments I don't recall starting, and having no odd cat-paw-driven random keystrokes. If I get ambitious I open the comment and then back it out, as trivial ghosts.

The closest SAP managed tag I could find for the creation/modification/freeze/off-lining of content on this SAP Community site was NetWeaver ABAP Data Archiving. It's related in general even if no R/3 system is directly behind community.sap.com. Individual posts, from a forbidden phrase comment to a marketing plant to a personal privacy removal mass request, can come and go via a published, shared, kind-of-well known methodology. Spaces and/or topic areas differ as they are deliberate group changes.

Social Sign-On Stuff

During the move to the current platform, certain content was to have been skipped over, as one might purge records past their retention periods. It got complicated from what I observed, and not as much went away as might have done. The recent freeze weekend for introducing another SAP product line exemplifies the usual acquire-assimilate-absorb process software vendors do, besides shipping code.

Forums Closed for Maintenance

The walled-domain responsibility assignments that have evolved for the SAP Community may be considered to cover a spectrum of time and interest on the part of topic space owners/curators. Maybe lots of questions come up and it's easier to point to a post than to tackle individual threads with repetitive answers. Maybe the team gets reorganized, and those who were active now aren't.   

People Dot SAP Dot Com Cannot Be Reached

How does it work? How should it work?

What I saw in the Information Architecture space was a "thank you, we're turning out the lights." I asked on back channels, as diplomatically as I could given the potential root causes, and didn't get clarity. Other than I could ask to have my draft moved to another space. I waited to see if anyone noticed the alert that was to have been raised when I asked to publish. Quiet since.

Announcing an "end-of-life" passage for community web content has to have specific process steps for maximum community awareness and recognition. If an SAP product line is shelved, no new features or releases for years, the content could be frozen then if necessary erased. It's happened to a few sites along the way, SAP Jam, the SAP community wiki (transformed into another appearance), and will keep happening as interest from writers and readers waxes and wanes.

I'll leave this 3 or 4 word per line non-haiku as my exit music.

 

#
 sunset post of SCN groups:
 birth, life, death, repeat
 code, ship, bugfix, repeat
 tune in, turn on, drop out
 post, comment, flail, quit

 

The title comes from the Firesign Theatre: “…but the sun is going down!” “Oh, no, no! You are confused! The horizon is moving up!” -- https://vinyldistractions.com/firesign-theatre-how-can-you-be-in-two-places-at-once-when-youre-not-anywhere-at-all/

entry.title: Cloud Transition for Sustainability and ESG enhancement
entry.content:
Embarking on a journey to the cloud is more than just a technological shift—it's a dynamic force reshaping our environmental impact, societal inclusivity, and governance standards. Imagine a world where our digital evolution not only propels energy efficiency, minimizes carbon footprints, and optimizes resources but also fosters a realm of accessibility, inclusivity, and robust governance. Let's dive into the transformative waves of cloud transition, exploring its ripple effects on our planet, our communities, and the ethical governance that guides our digital future.

 



Cloud Transition´s Impact on Environment


Green cloud transition significantly influences environmental aspects, focusing on energy efficiency, carbon footprint reduction, and resource optimization:

  1. Higher Energy Efficiency: Cloud transition enhances energy efficiency by optimizing data center operations, reducing energy consumption, and promoting the use of renewable energy sourcessignificantly boosting energy efficiency by up to 25%, according to Accenture studies.
  2. Reduced Carbon Footprint: By adopting green cloud practices, organizations minimize their carbon footprint through the efficient use of resources and the implementation of environmentally friendly technologies by up to 30%.
  3. Resource Optimization: Cloud transition enables better resource utilization through advanced technologies such as virtualization and containerization, leading to reduced waste and overall resource efficiency.

 

Cloud Transition's Impact on Social Responsibility


The shift to cloud computing also has a positive impact on social responsibility, fostering accessibility and inclusion for diverse user groups:

    1. Enhanced Accessibility: Cloud services facilitate universal access to computing resources, breaking down barriers and providing opportunities for users with varying needs and from different geographic locations.

 

    1. Inclusive Opportunities: Cloud platforms empower diverse user groups by providing equal access to information and services, promoting inclusivity and bridging digital divides.



Cloud Transition's Impact on Governance and Compliance


The transition to the cloud has significant implications for governance and compliance, affecting data governance, data security and operational transparency:

    1. Data Governance and Security: Cloud providers implement robust security measures, ensuring data protection and compliance with industry regulations. Organizations can benefit from advanced security features and protocols to safeguard sensitive information.

 

    1. Operational Transparency: Cloud transition enhances operational transparency through centralized management and monitoring tools. This provides organizations with a clearer view of their operations, aiding in compliance reporting and adherence to governance standards.



How to Migrate to Sustainable Green Cloud Computing?


Key steps include:

    1. Virtualization and Containerization: Reducing energy needs through dividing hardware into portions and promoting virtual infrastructure.

 

    1. Micro Data Centres: Lightweight, movable data centers that lower reaction time and decrease downtime.

 

    1. Adopting Rank-Based Microservices: Moving to microservices-based architecture and using containers for reduced energy consumption.

 

    1. AI-based Resource Scheduling: Efficient resource scheduling using AI to intelligently allocate resources and minimize power consumption.

 

    1. Effective Infrastructure Utilization with IaaS: Delegating infrastructure management to cloud providers for intelligent use of virtual machines and containers.



 



Conclusion


Cloud transition not only contributes to environmental sustainability by optimizing energy use and reducing carbon footprints but also aligns with social responsibility by promoting accessibility and inclusion. Additionally, the impact on governance and compliance is significant, with enhanced data security and operational transparency ensuring that organizations meet regulatory standards and ethical considerations in their cloud journey.

 

Joaquín Carballo

Sustainability Principal

M +54 9 11 2311 6947 | LinkedIn

entry.title: Dynamic Data Filtering in CDS Views Based on User Input
entry.content:
In SAP ABAP, Core Data Services (CDS) views provide a powerful way to model and consume data. However, one of the common requirements is to filter data dynamically based on user input. This capability allows users to view tailored data without requiring multiple static views. In this blog, we’ll explore how to achieve dynamic data filtering in CDS views based on user input and provide a complete coding example. 

Why Use Dynamic Data Filtering? 

Dynamic data filtering enables: 

  • Flexibility: Users can filter the results based on the criteria they choose at runtime. 
  • Performance: Only the relevant data is fetched, which reduces unnecessary data transfer and enhances performance. 
  • Interactivity: Applications become more user-centric by allowing dynamic input for filtering. 

What We’ll Build: 

We’ll create a CDS view that filters customer data based on user-provided input for: 

  • Customer ID 
  • Country 
     
    We’ll then expose this CDS view for consumption and demonstrate how to pass dynamic filter values through the view. 

Step 1: Define the CDS View with Input Parameters 

Here’s how you can define a CDS view with input parameters for dynamic filtering: 

Explanation: 

  • Parameters (p_cust_id and p_Country): These input parameters allow users to specify the customer ID and country for filtering. 
  • WHERE Clause: The filtering is done dynamically based on the input. If a parameter is not provided (i.e., is null), that condition is ignored. 
  • @odata.publish: true: This exposes the CDS view as an OData service, allowing it to be consumed via SAP Fiori or other front-end tools. 

Step 2: Testing the CDS View with Dynamic Inputs 

To test the CDS view, you can use SAP GUI or any OData client to pass the input parameters dynamically. Below is how you can do this: 

  • p_custid and p_cntry: These are dynamic inputs provided at runtime. 
  • lt_results: The filtered results based on user input are stored here. 

Once the CDS view is exposed as an OData service, you can query it from a browser or postman using the following URL: 

/sap/opu/odata/SAP/ZRH_CUSTOMER_SRV_01/ZRH_CUSTOMER_FILTERSet(Custid='0001000005',Country='IN') 

  • Custid ='0001000005' filters by the customer ID. 
  • Country ='IN' filters by the country. 

 Step 3: Sample Output Based on Dynamic Input 

Case 1: Input - Customer ID and Country Provided 

In this case, only the customer with Custid = '0001000005' and Country = 'IN' is returned. 

Case 2: Input - Only Country Provided 

 

 

Here, the filter is applied only for the Country = 'US', and all customers in the US are returned. 

Case 3: Input - No Input Provided 

When no input is provided, all customers are returned as the filter conditions are ignored. 

Conclusion 

Dynamic data filtering in CDS views based on user input allows you to create flexible, interactive applications that provide users with exactly the data they need. By using input parameters in your CDS views, you can build powerful, performance-optimized filters that adjust based on runtime inputs. Whether you're building an SAP Fiori application or an OData service, this technique enhances both performance and user experience.

entry.title: Re: I created a little helper UI5 App to check if you received your badges
entry.content: It is showing "Error Fetching Badges".

entry.title: Re: Advanced Intercompany Stock Transfer in SAP S/4HANA OP/Private Cloud
entry.content:
Hi

 

If the upstream requirement date changes does the PO date change?

The reason this was developed was to meet the IFRS audit requirements. ==> 2 questions:

Was that the only reason ? what is is missing from an audit point of view

what is missing in with the regular STR/STO functionality ? The STO has kind of 2 views within it. The purchasing view and the sales/delivery view 

 

Thanks

Robin

entry.title: Find an API I can use to get data from 'Manage Supplier Line Items' in S/4HANA
entry.content: I am seeking an API that can retrieve data from the ‘Manage Supplier Line Items’ module in S/4HANA. I attempted to use the sap/opu/odata/sap/API_SUPPLIERINVOICE_PROCESS_SRV/A_SupplierInvoice API; however, it does not provide the information I require. I have been informed that the invoices retrieved using this API are from the Supplier Invoices List, which includes invoices raised from a Purchase Order. It does not encompass other types of invoices, such as direct invoices.

entry.title: Re: Ad-hoc Service with Fixed Price billing or Billing Plan
entry.content:
Hello Gert,

ideally, the billing plan should be incorporated in the quotation, as it is clearly relevant to the commercial offer being made to the customer. It appears, however, that it is not possible to create a billing plan in a quotation. Is this correct? If yes, are there any plans to make this availalbe in future?

Thank you

Regards

Patrick

entry.title: Re: A Guide to Calling RFC Function Modules from SAP Integration Suite
entry.content:
Hi @venkat21b,

it is easy - you can see it on a screenshot right above the mentioned text. In short - just create new connection with type RFC.

entry.title: Re: Migrate the ASE database (On-Prem) to the SAP hana Cl...
entry.content:
Hi Namit,

Great you get the gateway working well on you side, glad to hear it.

Regarding your question, please check the different docs we provide in the Migration tool community page:

         https://pages.community.sap.com/topics/advanced-sql-migration

The one called "What SAP Advanced SQL Migrtion tool is" clearly states:

       "SAP Advanced SQLMigration cannot be used to migrate SAP applications, suchas Business Suite,ERP,ECC,BW...etc.between DBMS platforms. For this purpose,SAP already provides procedures and tools".

Can you tell me what you are exactly trying to do ?, are you moving and ERP system from ASE to Hana ?, if that's the case notice that you don't need to convert the data model, SAP is already providing the corresponding data model for Hana so that conversion is not required. If what you are trying to convert is an extension located in a different database then you can use this tool, but you probably will have to extract the data model to text files and then use extract_offline as you have indicated above.

Tell me the required details and we can continue the discussion if needed.

Regards.

Javier.

entry.title: Size Limit For Exchange Property in Content Modifier in SAP CPI?
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

I am curious about if there's any size limit for Exchange Property in Content Modifier in SAP CPI?

To be specific, to loop-getting data, I use Looping Process Call to get payload, and create a property (by using content modifier) in order to temporarily store and append data being fetched. The configuration of the property is as follow.

Action: Create

Name: StorePayload

Source Type: Expression

Source Value: ${property.StorePayload}${in.body}

 

I wonder how much data can I temporarily store in this property. (50MB? 100MB?)

 

Thank you in advance.

entry.title: Re: How SAP Cloud ALM Accelerates and Supports Digital Tr...
entry.content:
Hi Nilabh,

You have created this content in Technology Q&A, which is a "question and answer" the forum.
Your content has been framed as a forum question instead of as content to consume.

This content is more appropriate for the Technology Blogs by Members page.
In the future, please post such blog content in that area instead of in the Q&A page.

Thanks and regards, 
John
